content: The working memory capacity limits that constrain an AI agent's reasoning act as an unexpected functional analog to the intrinsic curiosity drive in reinforcement learning agents. Capacity limits force agents to selectively prioritize only high-uncertainty, high-reward information to retain in active memory, naturally replicating the exploratory behavior that curiosity-driven RL modules like ICM and RND are explicitly designed to produce.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
